title: Edge Cases and Irregulars
description: Learn the forms that do not follow the regular path: irregular plurals, special gender patterns, and verbs that change shape in surprising ways.

content.title: Irregular plurals
content.description: Some common nouns do not build the plural by simply adding a regular ending.

content.title: Memorize the high-frequency exceptions
content.text: The most useful irregular nouns are the ones you hear every day. Learn those first, then add the less common exceptions later.

content.title: Gender surprises
content.description: A noun ending can mislead you, so do not rely on the last letter alone.

content.note: The adjective matches neuter gender, not masculine.

content.title: Soft consonant changes
content.description: Some verbs and nouns change a consonant when forms are built, especially in older or higher-frequency patterns.

content.title: Common changes to watch for
items: т can alternate with ш in some patterns.
д can alternate with ж in some patterns.
к and г can soften before certain endings.
These changes often show up in derived forms, not in every tense.

content.note: Word-level exceptions often matter as much as the rule.

content.title: Irregular verbs and agreement
content.description: The most common irregular verbs are the ones you will use constantly.

content.title: Edge-case strategy
bullets: Learn the most frequent exceptions first.
Treat form changes as vocabulary, not just grammar.
Check agreement when a noun feels like it should belong to another gender.
Use examples to lock in the pattern instead of memorizing the rule alone.

content.question: Which noun below is an unchanging plural noun?
content.note: Unchanging nouns stay the same in singular and plural.
